2606.06467.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
2606.06467.paper_summary: Long-context inference in modern LLMs is increasingly constrained by decoding efficiency, especially in reasoning-heavy settings where models generate long intermediate chains of thought. Existing sparse attention methods often face a practical efficiency-quality trade-off. Structured block sparse methods typically provide stronger acceleration but incur noticeable quality loss, while token sparse methods are usually more accurate yet deliver limited end-to-end speedup because top-k routing over the full cache remains expensive. In this work, we propose cross-layer sparse attention (CLSA), which is built on top of KV-sharing architectures such as YOCO. The core idea is to share not only the KV cache across cross-decoder layers, but also the routing index. A single indexer computes token-level top-k selection once and reuses the resulting index across layers, thereby preserving the fine-grained selectivity of token sparse attention while amortizing the routing overhead. The resulting architecture improves all major inference bottlenecks jointly, including pre-filling, KV-cache storage, and long-context decoding. Experiments across short-context and long-context benchmarks show that CLSA is both accurate and efficient, achieving up to 7.6x decoding speedup and 17.1x overall throughput improvement at 128K context. These results suggest a more complete architectural solution for long-context LLMs that jointly advances model quality and inference efficiency.
2606.06467.paper_summary_zh: 現代大型語言模型中的長上下文推理越來越受到解碼效率的限制，尤其是在推理需求高的情境中，模型生成長的中間思維鏈。現有的稀疏注意力方法通常面臨實際效率與質量的權衡。結構化區塊稀疏方法通常提供更強的加速，但會造成明顯的質量損失，而令牌稀疏方法通常更準確，但由於對整個緩存進行 top-k 路由仍然昂貴，因此提供的端到端加速有限。在這項工作中，我們提出了跨層稀疏注意力（CLSA），它建立在如 YOCO 的 KV 共享架構之上。核心思想是跨解碼器層不僅共享 KV 緩存，還共享路由索引。一個單一的索引器計算一次令牌級別的 top-k 選擇，並在各層之間重用生成的索引，從而保留令牌稀疏注意力的細粒度選擇性，同時攤銷路由開銷。所得到的架構共同改善了所有主要的推理瓶頸，包括預填充、KV 緩存存儲和長上下文解碼。在短上下文和長上下文基準測試中的實驗顯示，CLSA 既準確又高效，在 128K 上下文下實現了高達 7.6 倍的解碼加速和 17.1 倍的整體吞吐量提升。這些結果表明，對於長上下文大型語言模型來說，這是一個更完整的架構解決方案，能夠共同推進模型質量和推理效率。
2606.06467.author: Yutao Sun et.al.
2606.06467.authors: Yutao Sun, Yanqi Zhang, Li Dong, Jianyong Wang, Furu Wei
